data.title: Validation blocked by missing commands.test_unit config — no graceful fallback or actionable error
data.description:
## What happened

In a host project, validation (validate.sh --ci) was blocked because commands.test_unit was not configured in dso-config.conf. The user had to manually fix the config and re-run.

## Expected behavior

When commands.test_unit is not configured, validation should either:
1. Fall back to a detected test command (e.g., from project-detect.sh results)
2. Emit a clear, actionable error: 'commands.test_unit not configured in .claude/dso-config.conf. Set it to your test command (e.g., make test, pytest, npm test). Run: echo commands.test_unit=<cmd> >> .claude/dso-config.conf'

## Actual behavior

Validation fails with an unclear error that doesn't tell the user what config key is missing or how to fix it.

## Fix needed

1. validate.sh should check for commands.test_unit early and emit a clear error with the config key name and example values
2. Consider auto-detecting the test command from project-detect.sh if the config is missing (project-detect.sh already identifies test frameworks during onboarding)
